title: Roblox Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Roblox delivered strong Q4 2024 results with revenue of $988.2 million, up 32% year-over-year, and bookings of $1.36 billion, up 21% year-over-year. The earnings call revealed the company's strategic pivot toward profitability while maintaining growth momentum. Management highlighted the successful expansion of the platform's user base to 82.9 million daily active users, up 21% from Q4 2023. Hours engaged reached 73.5 billion, up 23% year-over-year. The company reported a net loss of $221.1 million for the quarter, with adjusted EBITDA improving to $65.6 million. Management emphasized free cash flow generation, which surged 417% to $641.3 million for the full year 2024. Guidance for 2025 reflected continued strong growth expectations with revenue projected to grow in the mid-20s percentage range. The company outlined its vision for attracting older users and expanding beyond gaming into education and other sectors. Management discussed the competitive landscape and positioned Roblox as uniquely placed to benefit from metaverse adoption. Executive commentary suggested the company expects to reach profitability within several years as it scales revenue and moderates cost growth.
